scenarios.description: traverse(null, validRoot) throws IllegalArgumentException when visitor is null (Validate.notNull(visitor) fails)
scenarios.precondition: NodeVisitor visitor = null; Node root = new TextNode("text");

scenarios.description: traverse(validVisitor, null) throws IllegalArgumentException when root is null (Validate.notNull(root) fails)
traverse on single leaf node calls head and tail once then returns (no children, node == root)
scenarios.precondition: NodeVisitor visitor collects events; Node root = new TextNode("leaf");
scenarios.expectedResult: visitor.head called once with (root,0); visitor.tail called once with (root,0)
pseudocode: assert head called exactly once with depth=0; assert tail called exactly once with depth=0;

scenarios.description: traverse on root with one child descends then ascends (childNodeSize>0 triggers descend branch)
scenarios.precondition: NodeVisitor visitor collects order; Element root = new Element(Tag.valueOf("div"), ""); root.appendChild(new TextNode("c"));
scenarios.expectedResult: visitor.head called on root(depth0), child(depth1); visitor.tail called on child(depth1), root(depth0)
pseudocode: NodeVisitor visitor = (n,d) -> calls.add("head:"+n.nodeName()+":"+d); and then calls.add("tail:"+n.nodeName()+":"+d);
Element root = new Element(Tag.valueOf("div"),""); root.appendChild(new TextNode("c"));

scenarios.description: traverse handles replacement in head: parent child size same triggers replace branch
scenarios.expectedResult: no exceptions; traversal continues on replaced nodes in document order
pseudocode: NodeVisitor visitor = stub that replaces node in head;
Element root = new Element(Tag.valueOf("p"),""); root.appendText("a"); root.appendText("b");
assert no IllegalStateException; traversal visited both replaced nodes;

scenarios.description: traverse handles removal in head: parent child size changes triggers removal branch
scenarios.precondition:
// stub visitor removes each Node
NodeVisitor visitor = new NodeVisitor() { public void head(Node n,int d){ n.remove(); } public void tail(Node n,int d){} }; Element root = new Element(Tag.valueOf("ul"),""); root.appendChild(new Element(Tag.valueOf("li"),""));
scenarios.expectedResult: no tail calls for removed nodes; traversal ascends correctly and terminates
pseudocode: NodeVisitor visitor removes node in head;
Element root = new Element(Tag.valueOf("ul"),""); root.appendChild(new Element(Tag.valueOf("li"),"text"));
assert root.childNodeSize() == 0; no tail events for removed child;

scenarios.description: traverse on sibling chain visits all siblings in order (childNodeSize=0, nextSibling!=null branch)
scenarios.precondition: Element root = new Element(Tag.valueOf("div"),""); root.appendChild(new TextNode("a")); root.appendChild(new TextNode("b")); NodeVisitor visitor records sequence;
scenarios.expectedResult: head and tail called on first node(depth0) then head and tail on second node(depth0)

scenarios.description: traverse on deeper tree with multiple descendant levels (loop >1 iterations)
scenarios.precondition:
Element root = Parser.parseTree("
deep
"); NodeVisitor visitor counts entries;
pseudocode: NodeVisitor visitor = (n,d)-> depths.add(d) on head and on tail;

scenarios.description: traverse(elements) with empty list does nothing (iterator.hasNext() false)
scenarios.precondition: NodeVisitor visitor = (n,d)->{}; Elements empty = new Elements();
pseudocode: assert no head or tail calls;

scenarios.description: traverse(elements) with one element invokes child overload once
scenarios.precondition: NodeVisitor visitor count=0; Element e = new Element(Tag.valueOf("p"),""); Elements list=[e];
scenarios.expectedResult: visitor called on single element as in TC03; count incremented twice (head+tail)

scenarios.description: traverse(elements) with multiple elements invokes child overload for each in order
scenarios.precondition: NodeVisitor visitor records element tags; Elements elems=[e1,e2];
scenarios.expectedResult: visitor called for e1 then e2 sequences head+tail each
pseudocode: Elements elems = new Elements(new Element(Tag.valueOf("a"),""), new Element(Tag.valueOf("b"),""));

scenarios.description: traverse on node with multiple siblings and children covers both descend and ascend loops
scenarios.expectedResult: visitor events reflect correct depth-first order across branches
pseudocode: NodeVisitor visitor = (n,d)-> log.add("h:"+n.nodeName()+":"+d); and on tail add("t:"+n.nodeName()+":"+d);
assert log contains correct depth-first sequence;
